executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 370,022.76 inc VAT (340,421.20 ex VAT) for the week ending 06 June 2026.
Overall margin of 17.39% is under pressure and requires investigation given several departments are reporting data anomalies that distort the true P&L picture.
Total waste of 8,191.04 represents a waste percentage of 2.41% against ex-VAT sales, which is elevated and requires immediate department-level attention.

Multiple departments are reporting zero or missing purchases figures, resulting in incorrect or negative COGS calculations across Cigarettes, Produce, and Deli - these figures must be treated with caution and investigated with the purchasing/goods-in team.

The department P&L total shows a combined cost of sales of -127,906.48, which is clearly erroneous and driven by missing purchase data; true profitability cannot be accurately stated until purchases are reconciled.
Fish waste of 1,625.36 and Provisions waste of 1,016.80 are the two highest individual waste contributors after Produce (1,763.83), and all three require urgent review.

Deli scan margin of 42.12% is the strongest across all departments, indicating good theoretical profitability, but actual margin of 106.89% signals a data integrity issue with opening or closing stock.
biggest_wins: Beef achieved a reported margin of 83.73% on sales of 12,633.46, significantly ahead of its scan margin of 19.0%, though the gap warrants a stock count verification to confirm closing stock accuracy of 10,799.53.
Poultry delivered sales of 12,936.92 with a reported margin of 82.89%, well above its scan margin of 15.64%; if stock figures are validated this represents a strong trading week for the department.

Provisions posted sales of 24,532.33 with a COGS of just 198.20, yielding a reported margin of 99.19%; while the margin is likely inflated by missing purchase data, the department is clearly a high-volume contributor to the store.
Deli recorded sales of 8,464.12 with a scan margin of 42.12%, the highest scan margin in the store, indicating strong theoretical product mix and pricing performance.
Pork achieved a reported margin of 62.22% on sales of 1,363.07, outperforming its scan margin of 22.66%, suggesting good stock management and minimal shrinkage relative to the category size.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes is showing a cost of sales of -123,650.74 with a margin of 1,631.18% - this is clearly a data error caused by missing purchases; closing stock of 123,650.74 with no purchases recorded has inverted the COGS entirely and must be corrected as a priority.

Produce is reporting a cost of sales of -9,759.91 and a margin of 123.83% on sales of 40,960.14, again driven by missing purchases data; Produce waste of 1,763.83 is also the highest waste figure in the store and requires immediate attention.

Deli reports a negative COGS of -583.13 and a margin of 106.89%, indicating closing stock has increased beyond the level supported by purchases recorded; stock and purchase entries must be reconciled.

Fish waste of 1,625.36 is disproportionately high relative to Fish sales of 5,599.55, implying a waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 29% for the department - ordering, rotation, and date code management must be reviewed immediately.

Provisions waste of 1,016.80 is the second highest individual waste figure and is significant given the department's reliance on volume throughput; root cause should be identified across chilled and ambient lines.

Deli waste of 1,204.23 is the third highest absolute waste figure and, combined with the stock reconciliation issue, suggests production planning and end-of-day markdown procedures are not being followed consistently.

The total department P&L purchases figure of 0.00 confirms that purchase data has not been captured or allocated correctly across multiple departments this week; this undermines the integrity of the entire weekly P&L and must be escalated to the accounts/buying team before close of business.
Bread and Cakes waste of 764.78 and Poultry waste of 648.74 are both material figures that, if not addressed, will continue to erode actual margin performance across those categories.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 8,075.53 with no purchases recorded has produced a nonsensical margin of 1,631.18%; closing stock of 123,650.74 must be verified and purchases reconciled urgently - this department's figures are not usable in the current state.

Beef: Sales of 12,633.46, COGS of 2,056.02, reported margin of 83.73% vs scan of 19.0%; the large gap between actual and scan margin suggests a stock count discrepancy - closing stock of 10,799.53 should be verified; waste of 293.31 is manageable.
Lamb: Sales of 1,249.36, reported margin of 47.32% vs scan of 25.56%; margin outperformance is moderate and more plausible than other departments; waste of 100.36 is proportionate to category size.
Pork: Sales of 1,363.07, reported margin of 62.22% vs scan of 22.66%; as with Beef the gap is large and stock figures should be verified; waste of 154.17 is noted.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 1,010.73, reported margin of 87.34% vs scan of 11.91%; a 75-point gap between actual and scan margin is a significant red flag requiring purchase and stock data review; waste of 109.02 is recorded.

Poultry: Sales of 12,936.92, reported margin of 82.89% vs scan of 15.64%; strong sales week but margin gap is very wide; waste of 648.74 is high and ordering volumes should be reviewed to reduce end-of-week exposure.

Fish: Sales of 5,599.55, reported margin of 94.32% vs scan of 14.51%; margin figure is not credible without confirmed purchase data; waste of 1,625.36 against sales of 5,599.55 is a critical issue requiring immediate ordering and rotation review.

Produce: Sales of 40,960.14, missing purchases, reported margin of 123.83% - figure is not valid; waste of 1,763.83 is the highest in the store and a standalone action point regardless of the P&L data issue.

Deli: Sales of 8,464.12, negative COGS of -583.13, margin of 106.89% vs scan of 42.12%; scan margin of 42.12% is the strongest in the store and reflects a good product mix; however the actual margin figure is distorted and stock entries must be corrected; waste of 1,204.23 is high.

Provisions: Sales of 24,532.33, COGS of 198.20, margin of 99.19% vs scan of 19.2%; COGS of 198.20 on sales of 24,532.33 indicates purchases are almost entirely absent from the record; waste of 1,016.80 is a concern and should be broken down by product line.
action_plan: Escalate missing purchases data to the accounts and buying team today - departments including Cigarettes, Produce, Provisions, and Deli have zero or near-zero purchases recorded, rendering the weekly P&L unreliable; all GRNs must be reconciled against the system before the P&L is signed off.

Conduct an immediate stock count verification on Cigarettes - closing stock of 123,650.74 with no purchases recorded has produced a completely inverted COGS; confirm physical stock matches system and re-enter any missing purchase invoices.

Investigate Fish waste of 1,625.36 against Fish sales of 5,599.55 as an urgent priority - review ordering quantities, delivery frequency, date code compliance, and end-of-day markdown procedures; consider reducing order volumes for the coming week.

Review Produce ordering and waste management - waste of 1,763.83 is the single largest waste figure in the store; department manager to conduct a daily date code check and implement a structured markdown schedule to recover value before write-off.

Address Deli waste of 1,204.23 by reviewing production planning schedules - compare daily production output against actual sales by line and reduce preparation quantities on low-velocity products; ensure end-of-day markdown policy is being followed.

Investigate Provisions waste of 1,016.80 - identify the top five product lines contributing to this figure and assess whether ordering quantities, supplier delivery schedules, or in-store rotation procedures need to be adjusted.

Review Poultry ordering and waste - waste of 648.74 on sales of 12,936.92 is proportionally significant; ensure fresh poultry orders are being placed to match forecasted sales patterns and that end-of-week exposure is being managed through markdowns.

Review Bread and Cakes waste of 764.78 - liaise with the bakery supplier and department manager to align delivery quantities with actual sales data; confirm that day-old markdown and donation procedures are in place and being used.

Verify all closing stock figures for Beef, Lamb, Pork, Bacon, and Poultry - the gap between reported actual margins and scan margins across all fresh meat departments is too large to be explained by trading alone and likely reflects stock entry errors.

Request the OPR scan margin report for Cigarettes and Produce to cross-reference theoretical margin against the 12.82% and 19.47% scan figures respectively, and use this as a baseline to reconstruct the department P&L once purchase data is restored.

Schedule a department manager review meeting for the first half of next week to present corrected P&L figures once purchases are reconciled, and set individual waste reduction targets for Fish, Produce, Deli, and Provisions for the week ending 13 June 2026.

Monitor overall store margin closely - the reported 17.39% overall margin is likely not reflective of true performance given the data gaps; once purchases are fully entered, assess whether corrective action is needed on pricing, markdowns, or supplier terms.
